info.title: eCFR API Vocabulary
info.description: Domain vocabulary for the Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (eCFR) REST API covering regulatory hierarchy, search, versioning, and agency metadata.

terms.definition: Code of Federal Regulations — the official codification of the general and permanent rules and regulations published in the Federal Register by U.S. executive departments and agencies.
Electronic Code of Federal Regulations — the online, continuously-updated version of the CFR maintained by the Office of the Federal Register and the Government Publishing Office.
The highest organizational unit of the CFR, numbered 1 through 50, each covering a broad subject area such as Agriculture (Title 7) or Energy (Title 10).
An optional sub-division of a CFR title, identified by an uppercase letter (A, B, C), grouping related chapters together.
A subdivision of a CFR title or subtitle, typically assigned to a specific agency or bureau, identified by Roman numerals.
An optional subdivision within a CFR chapter, identified by uppercase letters, grouping related parts.
The primary operational unit of the CFR containing regulations on a specific subject. Parts are numbered and contain sections.
An optional subdivision of a CFR part grouping related sections together, identified by uppercase letters.
An informal grouping of sections within a subpart or part that share a related subject, identified by system-generated identifiers.
The basic unit of regulatory text in the CFR, identified by part number and section number (e.g., § 200.1), containing the actual regulatory requirements.
Supplementary material attached to a CFR part, subtitle, chapter, or title, identified by letters, Roman numerals, or descriptive names.
A U.S. federal department, independent agency, or sub-agency that authors and maintains CFR regulations. Agencies are identified by slug and linked to CFR chapter references.
A URL-safe, lowercase hyphenated identifier for an agency (e.g., 'environmental-protection-agency').
A structured pointer to a specific location in the CFR, consisting of a title number and chapter identifier, linking agencies to the regulatory text they administer.
An editorial or substantive correction to CFR text. Corrections are tracked with the date an error occurred and the date it was corrected.
A description of the change made to fix a CFR error, such as adding missing text, correcting a typographical error, or updating a cross-reference.
A Federal Register citation (volume and page number) identifying the official Federal Register publication associated with a correction or amendment.
The date on which a regulatory amendment became effective in the CFR.
The date on which a CFR amendment or correction was published in the Federal Register.
The most recent date on which any content within a CFR title was amended.
The date through which the eCFR data is current. Content after this date may not yet be reflected.
Indicates whether a regulatory change is substantive (true = meaningful policy change) versus non-substantive (false = purely editorial or technical correction).
A record capturing a specific state of a CFR section or appendix at a point in time, including its identifier, name, and publication dates.
Any node in the CFR hierarchy above a given section or appendix, from the immediate parent up through the title level.
The complete hierarchical outline of a CFR title, showing the tree of subtitles, chapters, subchapters, parts, subparts, subject groups, sections, and appendices.
An HTML snippet of the regulatory text surrounding a search match, with matched terms wrapped in <strong> tags.
A relevance ranking score assigned to a search result, used to order results by how closely they match the query.
A URL-safe identifier for an agency used as a search filter parameter (e.g., 'department-of-agriculture').
Human-readable heading labels for each level of the CFR hierarchy at a search result location, without HTML markup.
HTML-formatted heading labels for each level of the CFR hierarchy at a search result location, with matched search terms highlighted.
A flag indicating that a CFR title number, part, or section identifier has been reserved for future use but currently contains no regulatory content.
A flag indicating that a CFR section or appendix has been officially removed and is no longer in effect.
The eCFR API service that provides access to CFR content and structure at specific historical dates.
The eCFR API service that provides metadata about federal agencies, their CFR references, and editorial corrections.
The eCFR API service that provides full-text search across all CFR content with filtering, ranking, and aggregation capabilities.
The unique identifier for a CFR hierarchy node within its type — a number for titles and parts, a letter for subtitles and subparts, a Roman numeral for chapters, or a decimal string for sections.
The byte size of a CFR hierarchy node, indicating the volume of regulatory text contained within it.
The range of part numbers contained within a CFR subtitle or chapter node (e.g., '1 – 299').
